data_sources: 16 BOD cash forecast Excel files (Sep 2022 – May 2026): Historicals view KK, BU beta, Inflows Actuals sheets
description: Longitudinal analysis of Sears and Kmart retail store inflows — Sep 2022 through May 2026 BOD files

methodology.primary_source_monthly: BU beta monthly columns (col 22+) from 05.08.26_BU view file, corrected for 1-FY label shift
methodology.cross_validation: Historicals view KK confirms FY22/FY23 monthly actuals match BU beta. FY24-FY26 verified by summing BU monthly to match BU annual FY totals.
methodology.key_finding_on_data: BU beta monthly labels are 1 fiscal year ahead of actuals: columns labeled 'Feb 2024-Jan 2025' contain FY24 actual distribution; 'Feb 2025-Jan 2026' = FY25 actuals.
methodology.annual_source: BU beta FY annual cols 9(FY24), 11(FY25), 13(FY26), 17(FY27) from 05.08.26 BU view — these are labeled and confirmed
methodology.territories: No Guam/USVI retail inflow columns found — territory retail consolidated into main brand totals

FY22.note: FY22 predates the tracking period (files start Sep 2022). Annual total from BU beta.

FY23.source: Confirmed: BU beta monthly sum=203M + Historicals view KK monthly=201.4M

FY24.source: BU beta annual col 9; monthly from BU beta (shifted)
FY24.note: Sears collapsed 74%. This reflects large-scale store closures in calendar 2023.

FY25.source: BU beta annual col 11; monthly from BU beta (shifted)
FY25.note: Sears down further 51%. Kmart -21% but still $7.5M/month average.

FY26.source: BU beta annual col 13 (forecast as of May 2026); monthly from BU beta FY27 labels (shifted)
FY26.note: Kmart 5.4x Sears. Sears near extinction. User confirmed: May 2026 single month Kmart $0.80M vs Sears $0.01M.

fy_over_fy_changes.comment: Both roughly flat. Sears/Kmart B&M operations stable during this period.
MAJOR event: Sears store base collapsed. Large-scale closures in calendar 2023. Kmart held up far better.
Sears continued declining. Kmart steady at -20% YoY decline rate.
Kmart deceleration — only -7.4% decline, approaching plateau. Sears near residual.
Both projected flat to slightly up — Kmart operating at steady state for remaining stores.

monthly_actuals_and_forecast.description: Monthly inflows in $M. FY22/FY23 = confirmed actuals. FY24/FY25 = actuals (BU beta shifted series reconciled to annual totals). FY26 = forecast as of May 2026.
monthly_actuals_and_forecast.data_through: Jan 2026 actuals/forecast; additional FY26 months extrapolated

quarterly_inflows.description: Quarterly aggregates. Pre-FY26 = actuals; FY26 = forecast.

bm_anita_b_and_m_only_data.description: B&M Anita sheets in later BOD files track pure brick-and-mortar retail inflows (excludes Home Services, warranty, etc.)
bm_anita_b_and_m_only_data.finding: Sears B&M = 60% of total Sears inflows in FY23 (B&M $80.4M vs total $201.4M). Kmart B&M = 95.6% of total ($135.8M vs $142.0M). Kmart is almost entirely B&M.

FY24.note: Kmart B&M FY24 appears higher than total - may use different scope boundary

territory_analysis.conclusion: No separate Guam/USVI retail columns in Sears/Kmart inflow sections
territory_analysis.evidence: Scanned all 16 BOD files' first 30 rows for 'guam', 'usvi', 'virgin island' in any column header — found only in AP disbursement sections (government tax payments), not inflow columns
territory_analysis.implication: Territory operations consolidated into main brand totals or immaterial to cash forecast

store_count_analysis.explicit_counts: No explicit 'store count' or '# stores' rows found in BU beta/Historicals sheets
store_count_analysis.b_and_m_tracking: B&M Anita sheets confirm Sears/Kmart B&M operations tracked separately starting 2024
store_count_analysis.inferred_trajectory: Sears B&M revenue decline of 74% (FY23→FY24) implies large store closures. Kmart decline of only 7% (FY25→FY26) implies stable remaining store base.
store_count_analysis.per_store_note: Without explicit store counts, per-store economics cannot be computed from these files

key_findings.1_sears_collapse: Sears inflows fell 92.3% from FY22 ($198.5M) to FY26 ($15.3M forecast), CAGR -42% over 4 years
key_findings.2_kmart_resilience: Kmart declined only 41.8% over same period, CAGR -12.5%, and is plateauing at ~$83M/yr
key_findings.3_kmart_dominance_may26: By May 2026: Kmart 5.4x Sears. User confirmed single-month: Kmart $0.80M vs Sears $0.01M
key_findings.4_dec2022_holiday: December 2022 holiday peak: Sears $23.75M, Kmart $15.75M — last major holiday for both brands
key_findings.5_sears_crash_fy24: Sears lost 74% in FY24 (FY23→FY24). The ~$150M disappearance reflects the mass store closure program executed in calendar 2023
key_findings.6_kmart_plateau: Kmart approaching plateau: FY26 forecast $83M, FY27 forecast $84M. Remaining stores likely operating in minimal-cost wind-down mode
key_findings.7_retail_mix: Kmart is 95.6% B&M retail; Sears has meaningful non-B&M revenue (warranty/financial services) that cushioned apparent store-level decline metrics
key_findings.8_no_territory_inflows: No dedicated Guam/USVI retail inflow lines — territory retail immaterial or consolidated

data_quality_disclaimer: Monthly data for FY24/FY25 is derived from BU beta columns with a confirmed 1-FY label shift. Annual FY totals (52.20/25.66/83.06/89.68M) are sourced directly from labeled FY columns in BU beta and are fully reliable. All figures are in $M from data_only=True openpyxl reads.
